title: Copy-on-Write Process Memory Optimization
description: A sequence diagram showing how a parent and child process initially share the same physical memory pages after a fork(), and how a page is only copied when one process attempts to write to it.
relationships: Parent forks, creating Child. Both point to same physical pages (marked read-only).
Child attempts to write to a shared page, causing a page fault.
Kernel's page fault handler intercepts the fault, allocates a new physical page, copies the data, and updates the Child's page table for that page to point to the new copy (marked read/write).
The write operation then completes successfully on the private copy.
